why_problematic: Evidence directly supports claim as stated including its strength; no over-extension; no directional conflict; candidate_id traceable to strict_silver CSV.
Clean supported example (not used in V3.5 Table 2 but available).

Evidence directly supports claim; matched-prompt matched-budget framing matches; candidate_id traceable.

Claim says 'interpretation is resolved' while evidence says 'interpreting results demand sophisticated technical capabilities and domain expertise' — partial directional flip on a sub-component. A reviewer could argue this is contradiction, not mild.
Claim says PQN is 'recurrent value-based' but evidence says PQN is 'parallel-environment Q-learning' — factual mismatch on the same entity, could be contradiction.
Clean mild: claim adds 'as the main comparison basis' framing not in evidence; no directional conflict, no number swap; scope/precision over-extension only.

Claim says 'above 30% accuracy on broad cases' but evidence says '24.5% accuracy on comprehensive scenarios' — number inflation on the same metric. A reviewer would judge this as contradiction (detail-swap), not mild. This is the user-flagged T2 problem.

Clean strong_action: claim adds 'validation' (not in evidence) and asserts 'extensive expertise required' (operational/deployment framing). Direction consistent but strength excessive. No number swap.

Claim adds QMIX (not in evidence) and characterizes all methods as 'parallel-environment Q-learning algorithm' — could be contradiction (entity addition) or strong_action (operational characterization). Boundary nuance.
Clean strong_action: claim asserts 'reliability and collision behavior in one assessment' (operational framing) beyond evidence. Not used in V3.5 Table 2.

Cleanest strong_action case: evidence mentions numerical experiments only; claim asserts 'direct physical plant trials' — clear operational/deployment escalation beyond evidence scope. No number swap, no directional conflict.

Claim says 'above 60% accuracy' vs evidence '24.5% accuracy' AND '45%' vs '15.04%' — 2.5-3x number inflation on both metrics. A reviewer would judge this as contradiction (number swap), not strong_action. NOT used in V3.5 Table 2 (correctly excluded).
Clean strong_action case (not used in V3.5 Table 2).
Clean contradiction: claim says effectiveness is 'clear' and requires 'little expertise'; evidence says 'uncertain' and requires 'expertise'. Clear directional conflict on the same finding.
Clean contradiction: claim says comparison is with 'PPO-RNN and SAC'; evidence says 'IPPO and PQN'. Entity name swap (detail-swap).
Clean contradiction: claim says 'lower performance'; evidence says 'top performance'. Directional flip.
Clean contradiction: claim says training data 'already available'; evidence says 'lack of official training set'. Clear directional conflict on the same fact.
